title: What are God's works of providence?
summary: Professing WSC Q. 11 of the Westminster Shorter Catechism, grounded in Psalm 104:24, 27-31.
description: This household worship moves through Psalm 22:25-26 and Romans 8:29-31, and professes WSC Q. 11 — “What are God's works of providence?”, before closing with Psalm 104:24, 27-31.

elements.lede: Respond to God’s call to worship him by praying the Lord’s Prayer together:
elements.text: Our Father who art in heaven, hallowed be thy name. Thy kingdom come. Thy will be done on earth as it is in heaven. Give us this day our daily bread, and forgive us our trespasses, as we forgive those who trespass against us, and lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil. For thine is the kingdom and the power, and the glory, forever and ever. Amen.

elements.lede: Take a moment to “test and examine” your thoughts, feelings, and actions of the past day. “Return to the Lord” by confessing your sin (Lam 3:40).
elements.text:
Gracious God, our sins are too heavy to carry, too real to hide, and too deep to undo. 
Forgive what our lips tremble to name, what our hearts can no longer bear, and what has become for us a consuming fire of judgment. 
Set us free from a past that we cannot change; open to us a future in which we can be changed; and grant us grace to grow more and more in your likeness and image, through Jesus Christ, the light of the world. 
Amen.

29 For those whom he foreknew he also predestined to be conformed to the image of his Son, in order that he might be the firstborn among many brothers. 30 And those whom he predestined he also called, and those whom he called he also justified, and those whom he justified he also glorified. 31 What then shall we say to these things? If God is for us, who can be against us?

elements.title: The Riches of His Wisdom
elements.lyrics:
Oh the depths of the riches and wisdom
Knowledge and truth, hope and glory
How unsearchable perfect and loving
The One who answers to none
From Him and through Him and to Him belong all things
To Him be the glory forever and ever. Amen.

elements.text:
24 Lord, how manifold are your works!
 In wisdom have you made them all;
 the earth is full of your creatures. 
27 These all look to you,
 to give them their food in due season.
28 When you give it to them, they gather it up;
 when you open your hand, they are filled with good things.
29 When you hide your face, they are dismayed;
 when you take away their breath, they die
 and return to their dust.
30 When you send forth your Spirit, they are created,
 and you renew the face of the ground.
31 May the glory of the Lord endure forever;
 may the Lord rejoice in his works.
The head of the household may offer words of instruction, encouragement, and exhortation.

Make your ways known upon the earth, Lord God, your saving power among the peoples. Renew your Church in holiness and help us to serve you with joy. Guide the leaders of all nations that justice may prevail throughout the world. Let not the needy be forgotten, nor the hope of the poor taken away. Make us instruments of your peace and let your glory be over all the earth. Prayers for ourselves, our families, friends, and the world may be offered as the Lord leads.

O Lord, you never fail to support and govern those whom you bring up in your steadfast love and fear: Keep us, we pray, under your continual protection and providence, and give us a perpetual fear and love of your holy Name; through Jesus Christ our Lord, who lives and reigns with you and the Holy Spirit, one God, for ever and ever. 
Amen.

16 Now may the Lord of peace himself give you peace at all times in every way. The Lord be with you all. 
Amen.
